raw_consumer_statement: I have the power of attorney for my dad and I am on his banking accounts and pay his bills. He received a bill for his property taxes, which I paid on XX/XX/year> thinking I was using his credit card. I received a charge on my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX for {$3800.00}. ( credit card ending in XXXX ) When I called the card company to ask what this charge was for, they said it was for utilities to XXXX XXXX. When I told them I do not pay my utilities to XXXX XXXX they created a dispute and provided me with a credit, closed the account and issued me a new card. On XX/XX/XXXX my Dad received a noticed from the XXXX XXXX XXXX 's office stating his payment was denied because of a credit card dispute. When we saw the amount we realized this was the amount on my credit card. It wasn't for utilities like the credit card company told me, but it was for property taxes. I called XXXX XXXX and told them what had happened and they said they were going to charge my new card for this amount. I told them we had already paid the treasurer 's office with my dad 's card and they said they had to charge my new card ( ending in XXXX ) anyway and I would have to take it up with the company who receives double payment. I told them to cancel my new card then because they do not have my permission to send this payment. In XXXX I got my statement and they charged me anyway. I have contacted the XXXX XXXX XXXX 's department and they never received this second payment. They checked with their billing company " XXXX '' and they said they returned the payment to the credit card company. I contacted the credit card company and they said they never received it. Not only I have I incurred a substantial amount in fees, but now XXXX XXXX is trying to collect {$3800.00} from me when there is no agency that has this money. This is the timeline received from the Treasurer 's Office : We have of the payments and the debit to the parcel : The original payment was made : XX/XX/year> {$3800.00} ( includes {$91.00} fee ) XX/XX/year> -Dispute received by XXXX XX/XX/year> or XX/XX/year> funds returned to issuing bank The second payment was made : XX/XX/year> {$4000.00} ( includes {$94.00} fee ) This is XXXX 's response : The customer 's bank has the funds so the customer will need to make arrangements with their bank to get their funds returned to them.
